content: Cleave extension fully implemented at extensions/cleave/ (index.ts + assessment.ts, conflicts.ts, dispatcher.ts, planner.ts, types.ts, workspace.ts, worktree.ts). Registered in package.json pi.extensions. Skills/prompts still exist as legacy.
project-memory episodic memory: session narratives generated at shutdown via generateEpisode subagent, stored with embeddings for semantic search
project-memory uses Ollama (qwen3-embedding) for semantic retrieval; falls back to FTS5 keyword search if unavailable. See extensions/project-memory/embeddings.ts
pi-kit is a pi-package (extensions, prompts, skills, themes) for the pi coding agent. Root: ~/workspace/ai/pi-kit, package.json has `"keywords": ["pi-package"]`.

project-memory extension: SQLite+WAL storage (.pi/memory/facts.db), semantic retrieval via Ollama qwen3-embedding, working memory buffer (25-slot cap), episodic session narratives, background extraction via subagent, global knowledge base (~/.pi/memory/global.db), JSONL export/import for git sync, context pressure system with auto-compaction.

Cleave OpenSpec integration at extensions/cleave/openspec.ts: detectOpenSpec, listChanges, findExecutableChanges, parseTasksFile, taskGroupsToChildPlans, openspecChangeToSplitPlan, openspecChangeToSplitPlanWithContext, buildOpenSpecContext, parseDesignFileChanges, parseDesignDecisions, readSpecScenarios, supplementScopeFromDesign. Tests at extensions/cleave/openspec.test.ts. Wired into /cleave as fast path.

OpenSpec (Fission-AI/OpenSpec) is a spec-driven development CLI: proposal→specs→design→tasks workflow with delta-spec merge on archive. Core profiles: /opsx:propose, /opsx:apply, /opsx:archive. Expanded: /opsx:ff, /opsx:continue, /opsx:verify, /opsx:sync. Cleave integrates as execution layer for tasks.md. Repo: https://github.com/Fission-AI/OpenSpec

pi core compaction exposes session_before_compact hook — extension can provide custom compaction summary (extensionCompaction) by returning {compaction: {summary, firstKeptEntryId, tokensBefore, details}} from the event handler. See dist/core/agent-session.js:_runAutoCompaction()

project-memory compaction resilience: cloud gets first attempt via pi's normal path. On failure, onError increments compactionRetryCount and resets autoCompacted. Next tool_execution_end above threshold sets useLocalCompaction=true and retries. session_before_compact only intercepts when useLocalCompaction flag is set, generating summary via local Ollama model with 120s timeout, 60k char truncation, fileOps details, and num_ctx=32768. Resume relay uses pi.sendMessage({display:false}, {triggerTurn:true}) to avoid transcript pollution. Max 3 retry attempts per session.

pi-kit's only upstream dependency is `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` (the official extension API). All `@mariozechner/pi-ai` imports eliminated — StringEnum inlined to extensions/lib/typebox-helpers.ts. Runtime imports from pi-coding-agent limited to public exports: ExtensionAPI (type), DynamicBorder, serializeConversation, convertToLlm.

design-tree and cleave are bridged via design_tree_update 'implement' action: scaffolds openspec/changes/<node-id>/ from a decided node's content, then /cleave executes it. Full pipeline: design → decide → implement → /cleave → verify.

Design-tree extension refactored into 3 modules: types.ts (NodeStatus, DesignNode, DocumentSections), tree.ts (pure domain logic — parse/generate frontmatter+sections, scan, mutations, branching), index.ts (2 tools + commands + lifecycle). Agent can now autonomously create/mutate design nodes via design_tree_update tool. Documents have structured sections: Overview, Research, Decisions, Open Questions, Implementation Notes. Open questions synced between body and frontmatter. 'implement' action bridges decided nodes to OpenSpec by scaffolding proposal.md + design.md + tasks.md from the node's content. [2026-03-06]

OpenSpec extension at extensions/openspec/: types.ts (ChangeInfo, Scenario, Requirement, SpecFile, SpecSection, ChangeStage types), spec.ts (pure domain logic — getOpenSpecDir, ensureOpenSpecDir, listChanges, getChange, computeStage, parseSpecContent, parseScenarios, generateSpecFromProposal, formatScenario, generateSpecFile, createChange, addSpec, archiveChange, countScenarios, summarizeSpecs), index.ts (1 tool + 7 commands + message renderers). Registered in package.json pi.extensions.
defaults.ts content-hash guard: sha256 of last-deployed AGENTS.md stored at ~/.pi/agent/.agents-md-hash; mismatched hash triggers warning instead of overwrite. See extensions/defaults.ts.

Pi core model IDs use alias form without dates (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-6', not 'claude-opus-4-20250514'). Alias preferred per model-resolver.js comment. Bedrock uses 'us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1'. See dist/core/model-resolver.js.

docs/img/spec-pipeline.png: new D2 diagram showing design→propose→spec→contract→plan→implement→verify→archive pipeline with feedback loop. docs/img/architecture.png: regenerated with all current extension groups.

README.md updated: reflects current extensions with architecture/spec-pipeline diagrams. spec-pipeline.png added at docs/img/spec-pipeline.png (commit 73728d0). README references diagram inline instead of text pipeline description.

openspec/, design/, and .github/workflows/ directories initialized in pi-kit. openspec has baseline/, changes/, and README.md; design/ has README.md; CI workflow runs npm test on Node 20+22 on push/PR to main.
OpenSpecContext includes `apiContract: string | null`; buildOpenSpecContext checks api.yaml, api.yml, api.json (in that order, first found wins). See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.
openspec/archive/.gitkeep added so archive directory is tracked in git. openspec/ structure: baseline/, changes/, archive/, README.md.

TaskGroup.specDomains: string[] added to openspec.ts; parseTasksFile parses <!-- specs: domain/name, ... --> annotations on lines after group headers (only before first task). ChildPlan.specDomains? also added in types.ts. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts, extensions/cleave/types.ts.

ChildPlanWithDomains interface removed from workspace.ts — specDomains now lives directly on ChildPlan (types.ts). matchScenariosToChildren operates on ChildPlan.specDomains?. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts, extensions/cleave/types.ts.

matchScenariosToChildren returns Map<number, AssignedScenario[]> keyed by child index; every spec scenario guaranteed to appear in at least one child (orphan safety net). See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.

version-check extension implemented at extensions/version-check.ts: polls GitHub releases API hourly via setInterval, compares semver against package.json version, sends sendMessage notification on new release. Respects PI_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK and PI_OFFLINE env vars. Registered in package.json pi.extensions.
version-check.test.ts lives at extensions/version-check.test.ts (root level, not a subdir); requires explicit `extensions/*.test.ts` glob to be picked up by the test runner.

pi package-manager installGit supports `source.ref` for checkout on fresh clone, but updateGit always resets to @{upstream} branch — no tag-tracking on update. Tag-pinned installs via parseGitUrl (see dist/utils/git.js).

Cleave child processes are spawned as `pi -p --no-session` subprocesses with the prompt via stdin, working dir set to the worktree, and PI_CHILD=1 env var. They run a full pi agent with all extensions loaded. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

pi child processes support --model <id> or --model <provider>/<pattern> CLI flag with fuzzy matching via resolveCliModel() in dist/core/model-resolver.js — enables tier routing by passing model IDs to spawnChild().

buildChildPrompt() in dispatcher.ts extended design: skillPaths param adds 'Specialist Skills' directive with explicit read instructions per matched skill file; tiered execution loop wraps spawnChild with review phase using opus model via --model flag.

Pi TUI design investigation initiated: exploring pi-coding-agent TUI hooks for a live sidebar showing cleave/design-tree/openspec state. See /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/tui.md and extensions.md.
Pi TUI overlays support real-time animation (~30 FPS) via tui.setOverlay() OverlayHandle pattern; components call tui.invalidate() to trigger redraws. Enables live-updating status panels.

Pi TUI overlay system supports persistent side-panels via ctx.ui.setOverlay() with anchor/width/maxHeight options; responsive hiding when terminal < 100 cols. See /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/examples/extensions/overlay-qa-tests.ts command /overlay-sidepanel.
Pi overlay custom() components get OverlayHandle for external visibility control via onHandle:(handle)=>{...} option in custom() call; handle stored in outer scope for event-driven show/hide.

Pi overlay system persistent sidepanel pattern: ctx.ui.custom() with {overlay:true, overlayOptions:{anchor:'right-center', width:'25%', minWidth:30, visible:(termWidth)=>termWidth>=100}}; OverlayHandle.setHidden() toggled via onHandle callback. See /opt/homebrew/.../overlay-qa-tests.ts /overlay-sidepanel.

pi-tui OverlayHandle interface: hide() permanently removes overlay, setHidden(bool) temporarily shows/hides, isHidden() checks state. Lives at /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-tui/dist/tui.d.ts
pi-tui OverlayOptions: width/maxHeight as SizeValue (number or '25%'), anchor, offsetX/Y, row/col, margin (OverlayMargin|number), visible:(termWidth,termHeight)=>boolean. See tui.d.ts lines ~75-103.

ctx.ui.setWidget() accepts either string[] or (tui,theme)=>Component factory — only non-blocking persistent view mechanism in pi. Multiple widgets stack with placement option (belowEditor). See extensions/types.d.ts.
ctx.ui.setFooter(factory) replaces entire footer; factory gets FooterDataProvider with git branch + extension statuses + onBranchChange() callback. Available for dashboard use.
pi overlay custom() is always blocking (await ctx.ui.custom()) — keyboard input goes to overlay not editor. No truly persistent non-blocking sidepanel exists within pi's TUI model.

design node 'unified-dashboard' created at docs/unified-dashboard.md: exploring unified live dashboard for cleave+design-tree+openspec with 3 research sections (TUI API audit, current fragmented state, 4 architecture options A-D).
openspec/changes/skill-aware-dispatch/ scaffolded: proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md (3 groups, 31 tasks), specs/cleave/spec.md (7 requirements, 20 scenarios). See that directory.

extensions/cleave/review.ts implemented (518 lines): ReviewVerdict, ReviewIssue, ReviewConfig, ReviewRound, buildReviewPrompt, buildFixPrompt, parseReviewResult, severityGate, detectChurn, executeWithReview — full adversarial review loop. Tests at review.test.ts (545 lines).

extensions/cleave/skills.ts implemented (343 lines): SkillMapping interface, DEFAULT_MAPPINGS (python/rust/OCI/etc.), matchSkillsToChild(), resolveSkillPaths(), globMatches(). Tests at skills.test.ts (345 lines).

Cleave skill-aware dispatch fully merged to main: skills.ts + review.ts + dispatcher.ts + index.ts updated; 3 child branches (skill-matching, prompt-and-routing, review-loop) merged with conflict resolution. 2545 lines added across 12 files.
ChildPlan fields added: skills: string[] (skill matching), executeModel?: ModelTier (tier routing), specDomains: string[] (required, defaults []). See extensions/cleave/types.ts.

Cleave skill matching at extensions/cleave/skills.ts: SkillMapping interface, DEFAULT_MAPPINGS (python, rust, oci, k8s-operations, typescript, style, git), matchSkillsToChild (annotation-first then auto-match from scope globs), resolveSkillPaths, resolveModelTier. Tests at skills.test.ts. [2026-03-07]

Cleave review loop at extensions/cleave/review.ts: executeWithReview wraps child dispatch in adversarial review cycle. ReviewExecutor interface for testability. buildReviewPrompt (hostile reviewer), parseReviewResult (regex-based C/W/N extraction), severityGate (nits→accept, warnings→1 fix, critical→2 fixes, security→immediate escalate), detectChurn (normalized description comparison, Jaccard >50% threshold). ReviewConfig controls enabled/maxWarningFixes/maxCriticalFixes/churnThreshold. Tests at review.test.ts. [2026-03-07]

pi interactive mode maintains two widget containers (widgetContainerAbove, widgetContainerBelow) as Maps keyed by extension key; multiple extensions can each setWidget() independently — they stack vertically within the same container. renderWidgets() rebuilds container children on each update. See interactive-mode.js lines ~115-947.

pi footer: FooterDataProvider exposes getGitBranch(), getExtensionStatuses(), getAvailableProviderCount(), onBranchChange(). setFooter() replaces built-in FooterComponent entirely. Only one custom footer at a time (customFooter field). ReadonlyFooterDataProvider is a Pick of the non-mutating methods. See footer-data-provider.d.ts.

pi layout order (top→bottom): widgetContainerAbove → conversation area → widgetContainerBelow → footer. setWidget with placement='belowEditor' slots into widgetContainerBelow. Footer is always the last child of ui.

pi.events (EventBus) is accessible on the ExtensionAPI: emit(channel, data) and on(channel, handler)=>unsubscribe. Can be used for cross-extension pub/sub without shared-state coupling. See dist/core/event-bus.d.ts.

pi TUI layout order (top→bottom): headerContainer → chatContainer → widgetContainerAbove → editorContainer → widgetContainerBelow → footer. widgetContainerAbove gets a leading spacer when non-empty; MAX_WIDGET_LINES=10.

Cleave dispatcher.ts integrates ReviewConfig via dispatchChildren/dispatchSingleChild; review loop is off by default (DEFAULT_REVIEW_CONFIG.enabled=false); wired into executeWithReview per child. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

pi footer can render 2-3 lines (pwd, stats, optional extension statuses). If extension statuses are registered via setStatus(), they appear on a 3rd line sorted alphabetically. setFooter() replaces all 3 lines and must reimplement ~60 lines of pwd/token/model/context% logic.
unified-dashboard OpenSpec change scaffolded at openspec/changes/unified-dashboard/ (proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md). Status: decided, ready for spec writing and /cleave execution.

Unified live-view dashboard extension will live at extensions/dashboard/ (not extensions/view/). Design node at docs/unified-dashboard.md. Status: decided, being implemented via OpenSpec change at openspec/changes/unified-dashboard/.

design-tree extension emitter changes merged: updateWidget replaced with emitDesignTreeState writing sharedState.designTree; widget subcommand and widgetHidden removed. See extensions/design-tree/index.ts.

SharedState in extensions/shared-state.ts extended with designTree?, openspec?, cleave? properties; imports types from ./dashboard/types.ts; exports DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT = 'dashboard:update'. See extensions/shared-state.ts.
openspec extension emitter changes merged: emitOpenSpecState added writing sharedState.openspec after session_start and after change mutations. See extensions/openspec/index.ts.

extensions/dashboard/types.ts created with: DesignTreeDashboardState, DesignTreeFocusedNode, OpenSpecDashboardState, OpenSpecChangeEntry, CleaveState, CleaveChildState, CleaveStatus, DashboardMode, FooterState interfaces.

cleave extension emitter changes merged: emitCleaveState added in cleave/index.ts for lifecycle transitions; dispatcher.ts mirrors child state to sharedState.cleave.children. See extensions/cleave/index.ts, extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

setExtensionFooter in interactive-mode.js: removes built-in footer from ui, calls factory(tui, theme, footerDataProvider), adds result as child. Restoring to null restores built-in footer. footerDataProvider is the same ReadonlyFooterDataProvider passed to setFooter factory.

ctx.model is Model<any>|undefined with fields: id, name, provider, reasoning, contextWindow, maxTokens, cost. ctx.sessionManager.state.thinkingLevel available via session-manager.d.ts. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/types.d.ts
DashboardFooter.setContext(ctx) must be called on each event to keep token stats/model/context-usage current — ctx is not available at footer construction time. See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.

Unified dashboard extension (extensions/dashboard/) replaces status-bar.ts. Custom footer via setFooter() with compact/raised modes (Ctrl+Shift+D toggle). Reads sharedState written by design-tree, openspec, and cleave emitters. Three layers: Layer 0 (compact footer, always visible), Layer 1 (raised footer with section details), Layer 2 (interactive overlay, TODO stub).
unified-dashboard OpenSpec change archived (2026-03-07). Specs merged to baseline/: shared-state.md, emitters.md, footer.md, integration.md. Change at archive/2026-03-07-unified-dashboard.

extensions/dashboard/ implementation complete and smoke-tested: index.ts (setFooter + ctrl+shift+d + /dashboard), footer.ts (DashboardFooter, compact 3-line + raised 9-line modes, context gauge, token stats, model+thinking), overlay.ts (stub), types.ts. Renders correctly via jiti. Registered in package.json. status-bar.ts removed.

bootstrap.sh capability matrix updated: OpenSpec added as always-ready; Cleave shows 'partial' when no local models (qwen3/nemotron/devstral) are detected, 'ready' when local models available, 'missing' when git/node absent. See bootstrap.sh.

pi keybindings: ctrl+l=selectModel, ctrl+p=cycleModelForward, shift+ctrl+p=cycleModelBackward, shift+tab=cycleThinkingLevel, ctrl+o=expandTools, ctrl+t=toggleThinking, shift+ctrl+d=debug(reserved by pi-tui). Dashboard toggle uses ctrl+shift+b (available).

Dashboard Layer 2 overlay implemented at extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts: DashboardOverlay class (Component with handleInput) + showDashboardOverlay() using ctx.ui.custom() with overlay:true, anchor right-center, 40% width. Three tabs (Design Tree/OpenSpec/Cleave) with navigable lists, expand/collapse. Opened via /dashboard open or Ctrl+Shift+B from raised mode. [2026-03-07]

Dashboard extension at extensions/dashboard/: index.ts (setFooter + ctrl+shift+b + /dashboard [compact|raised|open]), footer.ts (compact 3-line + raised 9-line), overlay.ts (Layer 2 interactive 3-tab panel). Registered in package.json. All 3 layers complete.

DashboardOverlay item navigation: flat ListItem[] with depth/expandable/expandedKeys for hierarchical display; rebuildItems() on tab switch or expand/collapse; render() outputs bordered panel with tab bar + content + key hint footer. Progress bars rendered inline on expand for OpenSpec changes.
extensions/view/index.ts scaffolded but superseded by extensions/dashboard/ — can be removed.

Component interface in pi-tui requires: render(width:number):string[], invalidate():void. Optional: handleInput?(data:string):void, wantsKeyRelease?:boolean. No dispose() in Component itself — dispose is in the return type of custom()/setFooter()/setWidget() factory signatures. See tui.d.ts lines ~9-30.

ctx.ui.custom<T>() factory signature: (tui, theme, keybindings:KeybindingsManager, done:(result:T)=>void) => (Component & {dispose?():void}) | Promise<...>. Options: overlay?, overlayOptions?, onHandle?. Returns Promise<T>. See extensions/types.d.ts.

showExtensionCustom (interactive-mode.js:1335) implements ctx.ui.custom(): saves editor text, creates overlay or replaces editor, resolves Promise when done(result) called. Overlay path calls this.ui.showOverlay(component) and sets focus; close() calls this.ui.hideOverlay(). Shortcut handler runs async via Promise.resolve, no streaming guard.
truncateToWidth(text, maxWidth, ellipsis?, pad?) in pi-tui/dist/utils.d.ts — pad=true pads to exactly maxWidth with spaces. ANSI-aware via visibleWidth. Exported from @mariozechner/pi-tui index.

DashboardOverlay.setEventBus() wires live refresh from DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT; showDashboardOverlay now accepts optional pi param and passes pi.events to overlay; dispose() unsubscribes. See extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts.

DashboardOverlay.setEventBus() wires DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT for live refresh while overlay is open; dispose() unsubscribes (no eventBus field retained after unsubscribe). MAX_CONTENT_LINES=30 caps content before footer rows. See extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts.

DashboardFooter incremental scan fixed: cachedThinkingLevel updated in same forward pass as token stats via thinking_level_change entry check — eliminates O(n) reverse scan. See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.

extensions/dashboard/overlay-data.ts: pure data builders (buildDesignItems, buildOpenSpecItems, buildCleaveItems, rebuildItems, clampIndex, statusIcon, TABS, MAX_CONTENT_LINES, ThemeFn) extracted from overlay.ts. No pi-tui dependency — fully testable. Tests at extensions/dashboard/overlay-data.test.ts (27 tests).

extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts fully implemented: DashboardOverlay class with 3-tab interface (Design Tree/OpenSpec/Cleave), keyboard navigation (↑↓/Tab/1-3/←→/↵/Esc), progress bars, expand/collapse. Rendering logic imports pure builders from overlay-data.ts. showDashboardOverlay() anchors right-center 40% width.
docs/unified-dashboard.md has Usage Reference section: Ctrl+Shift+B shortcuts, /dashboard commands, overlay navigation keys, and display mode descriptions. Added at commit b5ad522.

@mariozechner/pi-tui resolves at /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-tui — nested dep, not globally installed. Other extensions (design-tree, 01-auth, openspec) also import value symbols from pi-tui and work via pi's jiti loader.

pi extension jiti aliases (Node.js mode): @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent, @mariozechner/pi-tui, @mariozechner/pi-agent-core, @mariozechner/pi-ai, @mariozechner/pi-ai/oauth, @sinclair/typebox. Set in getAliases() at dist/core/extensions/loader.js:56.
Tests run via `npx tsx --test extensions/*.test.ts extensions/**/*.test.ts`. Root-level glob required — `**` does not match top-level files. No build step needed.
pi footer.render() returns 2 lines (pwd+branch line and stats line). setFooter() replaces it entirely; custom footer must handle all display itself.
shift+ctrl+d is hardcoded in pi-tui tui.js as the global debug key (onDebug). No extension shortcut can override it. See /opt/homebrew/.../pi-tui/dist/tui.js:312.

offline-driver uses pi.registerProvider("local", {...}) to dynamically register Ollama models on session start — eliminates static models.json dependency. pi-ai's openai-completions handles streaming/tokens. The ask_local_model tool (local-inference) intentionally keeps its own HTTP implementation for sub-agent delegation.

User directive: pi-kit development is inherently spec-and-test-driven. Spec → tests → code pipeline. Cleave + OpenSpec must be fully integrated into all development patterns and skills as the default workflow. Assessment in progress.

Development Methodology directive added to config/AGENTS.md — spec-first is mandatory for cleave_assess complexity ≥ 2.0. Specs define what must be true; code is implementation detail. When specs and code disagree, spec is right.

API contract directive added to config/AGENTS.md: OpenAPI 3.1 spec must be derived from OpenSpec scenarios during Plan phase, placed at openspec/changes/<id>/api.yaml, before implementation begins. Given→preconditions, When→endpoints, Then→responses.
Planned: extend /assess spec (cleave/openspec.ts) to read api.yaml and include endpoint/schema checks in verification checklist — ~50 lines, option 1 of 3 for API contract enforcement.

Runtime OpenAPI validation middleware: pi-kit will prompt user at project-start/major-design-start whether to add middleware validation; guidance will be implementation-agnostic (mention express-openapi-validator, fastify schema compilation) with no language/framework position taken.

`/assess spec` extended to include full API contract validation: injects api.yaml into LLM prompt with endpoint/schema/status-code checks; formatSpecVerification adds contract conformance checklist. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.
pi-kit repo is public at https://github.com/cwilson613/pi-kit with branch protection on main requiring PR reviews.
GitHub main branch protection enabled: 1 approving review required, stale reviews dismissed, force pushes blocked, enforce_admins=false (owner can still push directly).

Root cause of cleave child spec gaps: taskGroupsToChildPlans splits by file scope; cross-cutting scenarios (e.g., RBAC gating) that span multiple layers fall between task groups with no child owning them.

Scenario-first task generation fix: (1) change /opsx:ff skill instructions to group tasks by spec domain not file layer; (2) auto-inject orphan scenarios into best-matching child with ⚠️ CROSS-CUTTING marker; (3) tasks.md group headers annotated with <!-- specs: domain/name --> for deterministic matching. Design node at design/scenario-first-task-generation.md (status: decided).

Scenario-first task generation OpenSpec change scaffolded at openspec/changes/scenario-first-task-generation/ with proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md, and 2 spec files (cleave/spec.md, openspec-skill/spec.md). Committed fc836e7.

Scenario-first task generation being implemented: matchScenariosToChildren() (exported) in workspace.ts performs 3-tier matching (annotation→scope→word-overlap) and auto-injects orphans with crossCutting=true flag. buildDesignSection now accepts pre-computed AssignedScenario[]. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.

matchScenariosToChildren 3-tier matching implemented and committed: tier-1 annotation (specDomains), tier-2 scope file reference, tier-3 word overlap; orphans auto-injected with crossCutting=true into best-matching child (scope→word-overlap→last child fallback). See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.
pi-kit version bumped to 0.1.1; v0.1.1 GitHub release published with scenario-first task generation changes. See CHANGELOG.md.
pi-kit version bumped to 0.1.2; v0.1.2 GitHub release published with version-check extension. See CHANGELOG.md.

Tiered execution loop for cleave children: opus/thinking for plan+review phases, cheap models (haiku/sonnet/local) for execution, iterate review→fix with thinking model up to configurable max iterations. Cost ~50% vs all-opus even with 2 review cycles. D4 promoted from deferred in design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.

Skill-to-scope auto-matching uses extensible SkillMapping[] with glob patterns (*.py→python, Containerfile→oci, *.rs→rust, *.yaml→k8s-operations); annotation override via <!-- skills: ... --> in tasks.md; both parsed by extended parseTasksFile. See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.

Skill-aware child dispatch design node at design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md (status: decided): hybrid scope-annotation matching, directive-style injection, ChildPlan.skills: string[], tiered execution loop (D4) with severity-gated escalation (D4a), diminishing returns guardrail (D4b), adversarial review agent (D4c). See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.

Tiered execution loop review phase uses adversarial /assess cleave posture: severity categories C/W/N gate iteration behavior; critical+security → immediate escalation; >50% issue reappearance between rounds → bail (churn detection); fix agent receives only review issues, not original task. See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md D4a/D4b/D4c.
Cleave+design-tree+openspec unified live sidebar investigation in progress: exploring pi overlay system for a persistent collapsible side-panel showing all three extension states simultaneously.

Recommended dashboard architecture: Option B — compact always-on setWidget summary (~3-5 lines) + Ctrl+Shift+D toggle overlay for interactive navigation. Widget updates live via tool_execution_update; sharedState for cross-extension data flow.
Skill-aware child dispatch design complete (status: decided). All Q1-Q8 closed. D1-D6 decided. See design/skill-aware-child-dispatch.md.
Skill-aware dispatch Q7 decided: review runs in same worktree (needs file context, not just diff). Q8: reviews parallelize across children. Q3/Q5: deferred. Q4: composable (multi-skill per child).
skill-aware-dispatch OpenSpec change fully marked complete (all 31 tasks checked off) at openspec/changes/skill-aware-dispatch/tasks.md — commit bc56746.

Unified dashboard D2 decided: footer raise/lower + overlay, no Zellij/tmux — three layers within pi (compact footer always, raised footer on toggle, interactive overlay on-demand). External pane deferred as optional future enhancement. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.

Unified dashboard D1 decided: decentralized emitters + custom footer panel — each extension emits in-memory state to sharedState + pi.events.emit('dashboard:update'); new dashboard extension renders custom footer via setFooter() with raise/lower. Absorbs status-bar.ts. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.

sharedState contract for unified dashboard: designTree (nodeCount/decidedCount/focusedNode), openspec (changes[]{name,stage,tasksDone,tasksTotal}), cleave (status enum + children[]{label,status,elapsed}). See docs/unified-dashboard.md Q1 research.

Unified dashboard D4 decided: live cleave progress via sharedState mutation from dispatcher spawn callbacks (~10 lines); pi.events.emit('dashboard:update') on each child state transition. No file-based IPC needed. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.
Unified dashboard D3 decided: Ctrl+Shift+D for raise/lower toggle (unbound, mnemonic). From raised mode, Enter opens interactive overlay (Layer 2). See docs/unified-dashboard.md.

Unified dashboard D5 decided: raise/lower IS the collapse; state persisted via pi.appendEntry('dashboard-state', {raised}), restored on session_start. Design-tree stops owning its own widget. /dashboard command provides explicit toggle. See docs/unified-dashboard.md.
unified-dashboard OpenSpec change archived to archive/2026-03-07-unified-dashboard; baseline specs merged. All 30 tasks complete (commit 2f14c20).
unified-dashboard tasks.md: all 35 tasks marked done including smoke test (task 7.4: jiti load + mock render verified compact 3-line and raised 9-line modes). Change archived.
Cleave /cleave command delegates planning to LLM via sendUserMessage with ask_local_model instruction — if ask_local_model tool is unavailable the workflow stalls silently with no fallback.

Cleave cleave_run merges all completed children sequentially with no conflict resolution between child branches — conflicts between siblings are only detected semantically (conflicts.ts) not resolved; merge failures are reported but execution continues anyway.

Cleave cleave_run tool silently ignores children whose worktree creation failed — they are marked failed but dispatch proceeds with no error surfaced to caller until final report. Failed worktrees mean those children are skipped entirely.
Cleave child process stdout/stderr accumulates unboundedly in memory — long-running children could exhaust memory (dispatcher.ts:118-119).
Cleave has no loadState/resume capability — state is persisted but a crashed mid-dispatch run cannot be resumed.

Cleave truncateHead import from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent fails under tsx (ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED) — works at runtime via pi's jiti loader but cannot be tested standalone. See extensions/cleave/index.ts.
project-memory linked minds incomplete — marked as "being rebuilt for SQLite store". /memory link command returns warning.
project-memory /memory edit opens editor but can't save changes back — shows "Direct editing not yet supported for SQLite store"

Cleave task decomposition loses cross-cutting spec scenarios when tasks.md groups are split by file/layer scope (models→service→integration) rather than spec domain. RBAC enforcement example: spec defined capability checks in create_session, but task groups split by layer left the enforcement wire-up unassigned to any child.

TaskGroup.specDomains must be accessed defensively (optional chaining / nullish coalescing) because test fixtures and manual callers omit it — openspec.ts uses group.specDomains?.length and (group.specDomains ?? []) spreads. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts lines ~237,595-597.

workspace.ts scope-match tier fixed: uses word-boundary regex (escaped filename dots) instead of substring — prevents false positives on common names like 'index','config'. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.
ChildPlan.specDomains is required string[] in types.ts; planner.ts defaults to [] for LLM-generated plans; workspace.ts no longer uses optional chaining.
workspace.ts domain prefix matching fixed with segment-aware domainMatches() helper (relay ≠ relay-admin). Committed 5ba2836. See extensions/cleave/workspace.ts.

pi-kit issue #5 open: `pi update` always resets to origin/HEAD (main), ignoring pinned tags — tag-pinned installs require upstream change to pi-coding-agent's updateGit(). Workaround: version-check extension notifies on new releases.

Cleave child process execution is opaque to the parent TUI — tool_execution_update only fires for the parent tool; child pi subprocesses have no IPC channel back to parent. Live dispatch progress requires file-based IPC or polling worktree status files.
mergeSmallGroups in openspec.ts deduplicates skills via Set but not specDomains — specDomains merge may produce duplicates when two groups share domain annotations.
buildChildPrompt skill detection uses string includes '## Specialist Skills' — a task description containing that phrase would false-positive. See extensions/cleave/dispatcher.ts.

openspec_manage fast_forward generates empty tasks.md when design.md already exists — spec scenarios not parsed into tasks. Symptoms: 'Parsed: 0 section(s), 0 scenario(s)' on add_spec; spec format mismatch with parseSpecContent parser.

resolveExecuteModel in dispatcher.ts: explicit executeModel annotation now checked BEFORE preferLocal override — explicit annotations always take precedence over local model preference. Fixed in dispatcher.ts:69-72.
planner.ts now defaults both specDomains and skills to [] at normalization — W1 fixed. Previously only specDomains was defaulted; index.ts patched skills post-hoc.

openspec add_spec parser expects ## ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED Requirements + ### Requirement: + #### Scenario: heading format; free-form specs (### S1: style) parse as 0 sections/scenarios and produce empty tasks.md on fast_forward. See extensions/openspec/spec.ts:211.

severityGate logic restructured: PASS→accept first, garbled NEEDS_REWORK/REJECT (0 issues)→escalate, nits-only (no criticals/warnings)→accept. Stale PASS_WITH_CONCERNS branch removed. See extensions/cleave/review.ts.
mapModelTierToFlag 'haiku'/'opus' use bare fuzzy strings; 'sonnet' returns undefined (pi default). Resolution unpredictable with custom registries. Comment added to dispatcher.ts.
executeWithReview skips review when initial execution fails (exitCode !== 0) — early return added after disabled check in review.ts to avoid wasting opus call on incomplete worktree.

parseReviewResult garbled output now escalates: severityGate returns escalate when status=NEEDS_REWORK/REJECT and zero issues parsed, rather than silently accepting. PASS_WITH_CONCERNS stale branch removed.

openspec fast_forward generates empty tasks.md when spec files use free-form headings (## Requirements / ### R1: style) instead of the required ## ADDED Requirements / ### Requirement: / #### Scenario: format — sections parse as empty, task generation loop produces no output.
PASS_WITH_CONCERNS with zero issues (no criticals/warnings) is accepted silently by severityGate — logically correct but could mask a malformed reviewer response. See extensions/cleave/review.ts.

cleave_run with 5-child plan (1 foundation + 4 dependents) only dispatched 4 children — dashboard-extension was silently dropped. Possibly a max_parallel=4 interaction where foundation child completed in wave 1 and only 3 of 4 dependents were dispatched in wave 2.
shared-state.ts comment still referenced 'status-bar' for context gauge — updated to 'dashboard'. See extensions/shared-state.ts.
design-tree emitter used (ctx as any).events?.emit?() but events bus is on pi, not ctx — fixed to pi.events.emit(DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT, {source:'design-tree'}). See extensions/design-tree/index.ts.

jiti smoke-test path confirmed: createJiti requires path.resolve('./package.json') as base (not '[eval]' or __filename in --input-type=commonjs mode). Use @mariozechner/jiti at /opt/homebrew/.../pi-coding-agent/node_modules/@mariozechner/jiti/lib/jiti.cjs.

jiti smoke-test confirmed working: createJiti(path.resolve('./package.json'), {interopDefault:true}) from @mariozechner/jiti loads pi-kit TypeScript extensions correctly. '[eval]' as filename throws ERR_INVALID_ARG_VALUE.
Ctrl+Shift+D is hardcoded in pi-tui as the global debug key (onDebug handler) — dashboard toggle binding was conflicting. Fixed to Ctrl+Shift+B in extensions/dashboard/index.ts.

Dashboard toggle shortcut changed from Ctrl+Shift+D → Ctrl+Shift+B because pi-tui hardcodes Shift+Ctrl+D as the global debug key handler (tui.js:312), which intercepts before extension shortcuts fire. [2026-03-07]
Dashboard index.ts stores tui reference as `any` — loses all type safety for tui.requestRender() and tui.invalidate() calls; runtime errors won't be caught at compile time.

Dashboard index.ts restoreMode() silently swallows all exceptions with empty catch — errors in sessionManager.getEntries() or entry parsing are invisible; persistMode() does the same. Debugging dashboard state persistence issues will be impossible.
pi-tui onDebug is wired in interactive-mode.js:1469 (this.ui.onDebug = () => this.handleDebugCommand()), not in tui.js — but the key binding shift+ctrl+d IS in tui.js:312.
tui.requestRender() and tui.invalidate() have no type definitions in pi-tui dist/*.d.ts — dashboard uses them via `any` cast with no compile-time safety, runtime behavior unverified.

extensions/cleave/index.ts: emitCleaveState() module-level function lacked pi param; fix imports DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT and adds pi.events.emit() call — C1 from adversarial assessment. See extensions/cleave/index.ts ~line 69.

overlay.ts imports TUI type from @mariozechner/pi-tui but the actual tui param in showDashboardOverlay comes from ctx.ui.custom() callback — the type may not match the imported TUI interface, requiring careful runtime validation. See extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts showDashboardOverlay().
extensions/cleave/index.ts: emitCleaveState() fixed (commit fa0905a) — now accepts pi param and calls pi.events.emit(DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT). All 7 call sites updated. C1 resolved.
extensions/shared-state.ts phantom FooterState re-export fixed (commit fa0905a) — now re-exports DashboardState (the actual type in dashboard/types.ts). W1 resolved.
extensions/openspec/index.ts: string literal 'dashboard:update' replaced with DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT constant import (commit fa0905a). W3 resolved.

Dashboard overlay fixes committed at 6a7a5a6: C1 empty-list guard, W1 error logging, W2 incremental token cache, W3/W4 dead ListItem fields removed, W5 gauge Math.ceil, W6 streaming guard, N1 ThemeColor types, N2 truncateToWidth, N3 design tree hint, O3 live event subscription, O4 MAX_CONTENT_LINES cap. Residual: 3 unavoidable `as any` for untyped session entries, no overlay unit tests, no SKILL.md for dashboard.

DashboardFooter had O(n) reverse linear scan for thinkingLevel per render — fixed by adding cachedThinkingLevel field updated during incremental entry scan, eliminating the redundant reverse pass. See extensions/dashboard/footer.ts.

overlay.test.ts superseded by overlay-data.test.ts (27 tests, pure logic, runs under tsx). overlay.ts itself untestable via tsx/node mock.module due to pi-tui import — covered indirectly through overlay-data.ts.

@mariozechner/pi-tui is nested inside pi-coding-agent/node_modules/ (not a top-level global package) — value imports like truncateToWidth, matchesKey, visibleWidth work via pi's jiti loader at runtime but fail when loading extensions directly via tsx or node. Dashboard fails silently because pi's extension loader catches errors without surfacing them.

Dashboard extension load failure root cause investigated: pi jiti loader aliases @mariozechner/pi-tui correctly (getAliases() in loader.js:56), so the failure is NOT a package resolution issue. Actual load error is silently swallowed in loadExtension() catch block with only error message (no stack). Root cause still unknown — may be a runtime error inside the factory function or an import of a sub-module not in aliases.

22 commits were unpushed to origin/main across multiple sessions (dashboard, OpenSpec, cleave skill-aware dispatch, overlay) — pi update silently used stale remote. Always push before testing with pi update.
Dashboard extension appeared broken ('not showing') because 22 unpushed commits existed locally — pi update pulled old remote. Fixed by pushing to origin/main (b5ad522).

01-auth pattern: testable domain logic (types, diagnoseError, extractErrorLine, ALL_PROVIDERS, findProvider, checkAllProviders, formatResults) lives in auth.ts; index.ts imports from auth.ts and re-exports for backward compat. pi-tui only in index.ts (renderCall/renderResult). See extensions/01-auth/auth.ts

model-budget.ts dynamic model resolution pattern: TIER_META defines prefix strings; findTierModel(ctx, tier) calls ctx.modelRegistry.getAll(), filters by provider='anthropic' and id.startsWith(prefix), sorts desc to get latest version. Avoids hardcoded model IDs.
OpenSpec scenario-to-OpenAPI mapping documented in skills/openspec/SKILL.md: Given→security/constraints, When→path+method+request, Then→status+response schema. See skills/openspec/SKILL.md.
CI workflow (.github/workflows/test.yml) includes `build-essential` + `python3` apt install step to support native npm deps on ubuntu-latest.

tasks.md task group headers annotated with HTML comment for spec-domain ownership: `## N. Group Title` followed by `<!-- specs: relay/rbac -->`. Written by generation step, read by orphan detector in workspace.ts.

skills/openspec/SKILL.md updated: scenario-first grouping guidance, spec-domain annotation examples (<!-- specs: domain/name -->), and full annotated tasks.md format documented. See skills/openspec/SKILL.md.
skills/cleave/SKILL.md updated: documents 3-tier annotation/scope/word-overlap matching and orphan ⚠️ CROSS-CUTTING auto-inject behavior. See skills/cleave/SKILL.md.
LLM-parsed ChildPlan objects in planner.ts normalized with defaults: scope??=[], specDomains??=[], dependsOn via depends_on alias. Normalization block at planner.ts ~140-152.
Cross-extension live state sharing uses globalThis via Symbol.for() key (see extensions/shared-state.ts). Pattern: write from one extension, read from another without import coupling.
tasks.md skill annotation: task group headers followed by <!-- skills: python, k8s-operations --> for explicit skill mapping; parallel to <!-- specs: domain/name --> pattern.

TaskGroup.skills: string[] added alongside specDomains — parsed from <!-- skills: skill1, skill2 --> annotation before first task; propagated to ChildPlan.skills via taskGroupsToChildPlans. mergeSmallGroups deduplicates skills via Set. See extensions/cleave/openspec.ts.

setFooter factory signature: (tui: TUI, theme: Theme, footerData: ReadonlyFooterDataProvider) => Component & { dispose?(): void }. footerData provides getGitBranch/getExtensionStatuses/getAvailableProviderCount/onBranchChange. Token/model stats available via ctx.sessionManager.

Dashboard data flow: each producer extension (design-tree, openspec, cleave) writes to sharedState + emits pi.events("dashboard:update"). Dashboard extension subscribes and calls tui.requestRender(). Extensions also re-render on turn_end, message_end, tool_execution_end events.
skills/cleave/SKILL.md model tier routing order corrected to: explicit annotation > local override > skill-based hint > default (sonnet). Matches dispatcher.ts fix in commit f8d2009.

showDashboardOverlay(ctx) wired in index.ts: Ctrl+Shift+B from raised mode opens overlay; /dashboard open explicit command. BlockContext via ctx.ui.custom() returns when user presses Esc. Commit 8385489.

Dashboard overlay triggered via showDashboardOverlay(ctx): Ctrl+Shift+B second press from raised mode, or /dashboard open. Blocking ctx.ui.custom() call returns on Esc. /dashboard compact|raised forces mode directly. See extensions/dashboard/index.ts.

jiti smoke-test shim pattern: Module._resolveFilename intercept + require.cache injection for @mariozechner/pi-tui and @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent; use path.resolve(process.cwd(),'package.json') as jiti base. Confirmed working for overlay.ts and index.ts.
DashboardOverlay.ThemeColor casts: variable should be typed as ThemeColor directly rather than cast via `as any`. Pattern: always type ThemeColor variables at declaration, not cast at use-site.

DashboardFooter incremental scan pattern: both token stats and thinkingLevel are cached and updated together in a single forward pass over new entries (lastEntryCount..length), never re-scanning old entries.

Dashboard overlay testability pattern: extract pure data builders into overlay-data.ts (no pi-tui imports), test those directly under tsx; overlay.ts imports builders and handles only TUI rendering concerns.

ThemeFn bridge pattern: overlay-data.ts uses ThemeFn=(color:string,text:string)=>string for testability; overlay.ts bridges to pi-tui via `color as any` cast — intentional adapter at pure-logic/pi-tui boundary.

OCI skill created: skills/oci/SKILL.md — Containerfile conventions, multi-arch builds, registry auth (GHCR/ECR/Docker Hub), tagging strategy, security scanning, OCI artifacts (Helm/cosign/oras), CI/CD integration, common gotchas. Committed to pi-kit.
682 tests passing pre-workspace.test.ts addition; new tests added for annotation parsing (openspec.test.ts) and matchScenariosToChildren (workspace.test.ts) — total count not yet re-run.
openspec.test.ts extended with 4 annotation-parsing tests (specDomains field: single domain, multiple domains, missing annotation, annotation after tasks started).

workspace.test.ts at extensions/cleave/workspace.test.ts: 12 tests covering matchScenariosToChildren (all 3 tiers, orphan injection, scope routing, last-child fallback, segment-aware domain matching, word-boundary scope matching).
852/852 tests passing after dashboard overlay-data tests added (27 new). Run via `npx tsx --test extensions/*.test.ts extensions/**/*.test.ts`.

title: Seeded Project Memory with pi-kit Architecture Facts
narrative: The goal was to populate pi-kit's project-memory extension with foundational facts so future sessions start with context pre-loaded. Background extraction had already auto-captured 14 facts from the conversation; additional facts were manually stored covering known issues (linked minds incomplete, /memory edit broken), patterns (skills inventory), and constraints (test invocation). Four redundant overlapping facts were archived to reduce noise. The memory store now holds 18 facts across Architecture, Known Issues, Patterns & Conventions, and Constraints sections, ready for injection on the next session's first turn.

title: Fixed /exit command returning to REPL prompt instead of exiting
narrative: The goal was to fix a bug where the `/exit` command in project-memory extension would show the 'Memory saved' notification but then return control to the REPL prompt instead of exiting. Root cause was that `ctx.shutdown()` is fire-and-forget in interactive mode — it sets a flag and calls `void this.shutdown()`, returning immediately rather than blocking. The fix keeps the command handler alive after calling `ctx.shutdown()` by blocking on a timeout promise, so `process.exit(0)` fires during the wait rather than control returning to the prompt. A safety-net force-exit was added initially at 30s then reduced to 10s at the user's request. Both code paths (store present and store absent) were fixed and committed.

title: Disabled duplicate coe-agent status bar extension
narrative: The goal was to identify why a duplicate context bar (showing token usage with block characters) was appearing in the pi status bar. Investigation traced it to a recro `coe-agent.ts` extension loaded from `~/.pi/agent/git/github.com/recro/coe-agent/`, which registered its own `"coe"` status segment duplicating what pi-kit's `status-bar.ts` already provides. The fix was to rename the coe-agent directory to `coe-agent.disabled` so pi no longer discovers it as a package. No open threads; user should restart pi to confirm the duplicate bar is gone.

title: Fixed project-memory compaction resilience after adversarial review
narrative: The goal was to address critical and warning-level issues from an adversarial review of the project-memory extension's compaction fallback logic. Key fixes included: making session_before_compact a true fallback (only intercepting when useLocalCompaction flag is set after cloud failure, not unconditionally), replacing sendUserMessage with pi.sendMessage({display:false}) to prevent phantom transcript messages, adding AbortSignal timeout (120s) on Ollama inference calls, adding 60k char conversation truncation with num_ctx:32768, fixing embedding model filtering, and adding retry backoff with a configurable limit. Supporting config options (compactionLocalFallback, compactionLocalTimeout, compactionRetryLimit) were added to types.ts. All changes were committed and pushed to origin/main as 199e0b4. Open threads include missing tests and duplicated prompt constants that can't be fixed without pi exporting them.

title: Generated Space Marine portrait using mflux locally
narrative: The user wanted a photo of their friend transformed into a Warhammer 40K Space Marine. mflux was not installed, so the assistant set it up by initializing a uv project and installing mflux. A portrait was then generated depicting the man in Ultramarines power armor with gold trim and imperial iconography. The image was saved locally; no code changes were committed since this was purely an image generation task.

title: Refactored offline-driver to use pi.registerProvider() for Ollama
narrative: The goal was to eliminate the static models.json dependency in the offline-driver extension by leveraging pi-ai's official provider registration API. The offline-driver was refactored to discover available Ollama models dynamically via /api/tags on session start and register them through pi.registerProvider(), letting pi-ai handle all streaming and token tracking internally. The decision was made to keep the local-inference extension's own HTTP implementation since it serves a different purpose (sub-agent delegation where Claude remains the driver). All upstream @mariozechner/pi-ai imports were previously cleaned up by inlining StringEnum; this commit completes the decoupling work. Changes were committed and pushed; no open threads remain.

title: Tested Hugging Face API endpoints without token
narrative: The goal was to test connectivity to a Hugging Face inference endpoint. No HF token was found in the environment, keychain, or cache, so the attempt proceeded using public/unauthenticated access. The public model listing API responded successfully (HTTP 200), but all inference endpoints—including the newly required router.huggingface.co—returned 401 Unauthorized, with the legacy api-inference.huggingface.co returning 410 Gone. The session concluded that an HF_TOKEN is now mandatory for inference; next step is configuring one via secrets.

title: Demo of multi-tier inference chaining across four sources
narrative: The user asked for a live demonstration of chaining multiple inference sources together. The session explored the HF router endpoint briefly, then pivoted to a structured demo: Haiku generated an outline, a local Llama 3.1 8B model via Ollama drafted prose at zero API cost, Opus rewrote it with domain expertise, and Sonnet orchestrated the full flow. The demo successfully showed cost-aware routing across cloud tiers and local inference, though local image generation (FLUX.1/mflux) failed because diffusion-cli was not installed. An open thread is setting up the diffusion-cli environment to enable fully offline image generation.

title: Fixed JSONL import/export merge=union resilience in factstore
narrative: The goal was to make the project-memory FactStore's JSONL import/export pipeline resilient to git merge=union producing duplicate lines. Key bugs fixed: episode import was generating new nanoid IDs instead of preserving exported IDs (breaking dedup), nested transaction crashes were possible from storeEpisode inside importFromJsonl, and export ordering was non-deterministic. The fix extracted _storeEpisodeInner() to share between storeEpisode and import, added pre-dedup logic that keeps the highest reinforcement_count on conflicting fact lines, and added id tie-breaking to all export ORDER BY clauses. Eight new tests were added covering fact/episode/edge dedup, cross-machine sync, and determinism. All 174 tests pass; changes committed to main as a31e508.

title: Dashboard extension smoke tested and OpenSpec archived
narrative: The goal was to validate the newly built unified dashboard extension by loading all its files through jiti and rendering both display modes with mock data. All four dashboard files (shared-state, types, footer, index) loaded successfully via jiti. Mock renders confirmed compact mode (3 lines with context gauge, token stats, model/thinking indicator) and raised mode (9 lines with design tree, openspec, and live cleave dispatch sections) both work correctly within the 10-line cap. With all 30 tasks verified complete, the OpenSpec 'unified-dashboard' change was archived to baseline and the design tree focus cleared, leaving the Layer 2 overlay as the only remaining future work.

title: Fixed dashboard shortcut shadowed by pi-tui debug key
narrative: The dashboard toggle shortcut `Ctrl+Shift+D` was never firing because pi-tui hardcodes that key combination as a global debug handler (tui.js:312), intercepting it before any extension shortcut could receive it. The fix was to switch the dashboard toggle binding to `Ctrl+Shift+B`, which was confirmed unused across the pi-coding-agent codebase. Two source files were updated (`extensions/dashboard/index.ts` and `extensions/dashboard/overlay.ts`) and the reason was stored in project memory. The `/dashboard` slash-command fallback continues to work unchanged.

title: Fixed Cleave Dashboard Event Emission and Type Cleanup
narrative: The goal was to fix bugs in the pi-kit dashboard event system where cleave state transitions were being written to shared state without firing the notification event, causing the dashboard to never re-render. Three fixes were applied: C1 refactored `emitCleaveState` to accept the `pi` instance and emit `DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT` across all 7 call sites; W1 corrected a phantom `FooterState` re-export in `shared-state.ts` to the actual `DashboardState` type; and W3 made OpenSpec use the `DASHBOARD_UPDATE_EVENT` constant instead of a raw string literal for consistency. All 825 tests continued passing, and changes were committed to main as `fa0905a`. Three pre-existing issues were noted but left untouched: a `CleaveState` name collision (intentional separation), flat scenario handling in `formatSpecVerification`, and string detection in `buildChildPrompt`.

title: Dashboard Extension Review Items All Resolved
narrative: The goal was to close out all adversarial review findings against the pi-kit dashboard extension. All 16+ items were resolved across two commits: critical issues (selectedIndex guard, error swallowing, streaming overlay guard), warnings (O(n) scans, dead fields, gauge inflation), and nitpicks (typed ThemeColor casts, truncation consistency, empty tab hint). A key architectural decision was extracting pure logic into overlay-data.ts to enable testability without the pi-tui dependency, with an intentional `as any` adapter at the boundary. The session ended with 852/852 tests passing, a clean working tree on main, and memory facts already reflecting the incremental thinkingLevel cache fix. No open threads remain.

title: Diagnosed and pushed 22 unpushed commits to main
narrative: The session was investigating why the dashboard extension failed to load after a `pi update`, with errors indicating `@mariozechner/pi-tui` couldn't be resolved. After ruling out module resolution issues, the root cause was identified: 22 commits existed locally but had never been pushed to the remote, meaning `pi update` pulled an older version without any dashboard work. All 22 commits were pushed to `main` successfully, and the user was advised to run `pi update` again to get the dashboard extension and related changes.
